tasks.system: You are a senior vehicle-wrap production designer writing a complete, no-ambiguity spec sheet for a wrap installer (Tuthill Design + a regional vinyl shop). Output clean, structured Markdown. No marketing language. Every measurement has a unit. Every color has a hex code. Every asset has a size and a position. The installer must be able to cut and apply without asking a single question.
tasks.prompt:
Write a complete vehicle-wrap production specification for Big Muddy Touring's tour van.

## Vehicle
- 2014 Ford Transit 350 XLT
- High-roof variant (often called 'high top')
- Extended length / long wheelbase (148" wheelbase, ~263" overall length, ~84" overall height)
- Cargo van configuration (no rear or side cargo windows)

## Brand context
- Brand: Big Muddy Touring (the live-music + booking arm of Big Muddy / MBT)
- Voice: rugged, classic American, blues-corridor, lived-in. Not luxury, not corporate, not cute.
- Geographic origin: Natchez, Mississippi (Lower Mississippi corridor)
- Sister properties whose logos appear on the rear: Big Muddy Inn, Big Muddy Magazine, Big Muddy Radio, Big Muddy Records, Deep South Directory, Outsider Economics, Bearsville Creative, Chase Pierson Photography

## Design intent
- Base: full-body matte black vinyl wrap (3M 1080-M12 Deep Black or equivalent matte cast vinyl)
- Hex reference for renders: #0a0a0a
- Single accent color: warm gold (Big Muddy brand accent), hex #c8943e
- Wordmark zones: cream/beige base for visibility against matte black, hex #e8e0d4 (placeholder color until Tuthill delivers final wordmark on transparent vinyl)

## Required outputs in this spec

1. **Materials list** — exact vinyl SKUs (3M 1080-M12 base, 3M brand for accent + cut-vinyl wordmarks, application tape, primer/edge sealer, lamination film if needed). Quantities for a 2014 Transit 350 EL high roof.

2. **Per-panel layout specs**, one section each for: Driver Side, Passenger Side, Rear Cargo Doors (left + right as a pair), Front Cab + Grille, Roof.
   For each panel:
   - Total panel dimensions (in inches)
   - List of every wrap element on that panel with: element name, dimensions (W x H), top-left anchor coordinate measured from the panel's top-left corner (x, y), vinyl color, notes
   - Where the wordmark goes (size + anchor) — placeholder text 'BIG MUDDY TOURING' indicated; Tuthill supplies final art
   - Where the gold hairline runs (1" wide, full panel length, anchor y)

3. **Rear cargo doors specifically**:
   - Top zone: 'BIG MUDDY TOURING' wordmark (~24" tall, centered, ~6" from top)
   - Middle zone: 4×2 grid of property logos. Tile size: 8" x 8" each. 2" gutters between tiles. Center the grid horizontally. Logos in this order (left-to-right, top-to-bottom): Big Muddy Inn, Big Muddy Magazine, Big Muddy Radio, Big Muddy Records, Deep South Directory, Outsider Economics, Bearsville Creative, Chase Pierson Photography. Each tile cream-base with the property logo cut in matte black (or supplied as printed sticker).
   - Bottom zone: two QR codes, 4" x 4" each, ~6" from bottom, ~12" in from each outside edge. Left QR labeled 'TOUR / BOOK A SHOW' (resolves to bigmuddytouring.com/book), right QR labeled 'BLUES ROOM / TICKETS' (resolves to bigmuddytouring.com/shows). QR codes printed on cream tile with high-contrast black modules.

4. **Production notes**
   - Wrap install order (panels first, accents second, cut-vinyl text last)
   - Heat-gun temperatures + edge-sealer recommendations
   - QR code spec: minimum module size for scan-from-15ft reliability, error-correction level recommendation (likely H), URL-shortener strategy (use canonical bigmuddytouring.com URLs, not bit.ly)
   - Maintenance: matte vinyl care notes, expected lifespan (5-7 years for 1080-M12)

5. **Cost estimate** — rough total in USD for materials + 2-day pro install at a regional shop (Memphis/Jackson/NOLA). Show line items.

6. **Open questions for Tuthill** — anything the spec leaves to her judgment (final wordmark proportions, monogram on hood yes/no, any tone-on-tone matte-on-matte details, license plate frame, etc.)

Deliver the entire spec in clean markdown with H2 sections per panel. Use tables where they help. No fluff.

tasks.system: You are a senior prompt engineer for Google Imagen 3 image generation. You know Imagen 3's strengths (photorealism, lighting, materials, composition) and weaknesses (text rendering, fine logos, exact vehicle make/model accuracy). Your job is to produce optimized prompts that maximize the model's strengths and explicitly route around its weaknesses. Output clean Markdown.
tasks.prompt:
Produce three optimized Imagen 3 prompts to render photorealistic mockups of Big Muddy Touring's wrapped tour van. Same vehicle, same wrap design, three different camera angles for marketing/social.

## Source spec
- Vehicle: 2014 Ford Transit 350 XLT, high-roof, extended length (long wheelbase), cargo van (no side or rear windows)
- Wrap: full matte black base (#0a0a0a), warm gold hairline accent (#c8943e), cream wordmark/logo placeholder zones (#e8e0d4)
- DO NOT attempt to render any text, words, letters, logos, or QR codes. Indicate placeholder zones as clean blank cream-colored rectangular panels suitable for post-production overlay.
- Setting: Lower Mississippi corridor (Natchez, MS region). Likely backdrops: gravel lot in front of an old wooden juke joint at dusk; bluff overlooking the Mississippi River at golden hour; an empty Main Street in Natchez, brick storefronts, late afternoon light.

## Mood
- Rugged, lived-in, classic American touring van
- Cinematic, warm-shadowed, slightly cool-highlighted, mid-grain film aesthetic
- Not luxury, not theatrical, not 'Instagram-shiny'

## Three angles required

1. **Hero shot** — 3/4 angle from rear-right, showing passenger side and rear cargo doors. Dusk, juke joint backdrop. This is the marketing hero image.

2. **Profile shot** — pure side profile of the passenger side, full vehicle in frame. Natchez bluff backdrop, late golden hour. This shows the wordmark zone clearly for editorial use.

3. **Rear shot** — straight-on view of rear cargo doors, slightly low angle, moderate distance (~15 ft). Daylight, plain warehouse exterior backdrop, even lighting. This is for the QR-codes-and-property-logos production reference photo (so the team can verify scanner-friendliness once real codes are dropped in).

## Output format

For EACH of the three prompts, provide:

- **The prompt itself** as a single block of text ready to paste into Imagen 3
- **Negative prompt** — what to explicitly exclude (no text, no Mercedes Sprinter, no luxury van, no chrome trim, no glossy finish, no logos, no people in foreground, no other vehicles, no signage that competes with the van)
- **Aspect ratio** recommendation (probably 16:9 for hero, 3:2 for profile, 1:1 for rear)
- **Generation settings** — guidance scale, sample count recommendation
- **Why this works for Imagen 3 specifically** — short note on which prompt patterns Imagen 3 responds best to (e.g., explicit camera/lens specs, named lighting setups, material descriptors)
- **Likely failure modes** — what to look for when reviewing the generated images (e.g., wrong vehicle make if you don't anchor to Ford Transit's specific silhouette features; spurious text appearing in placeholder zones; reflections that betray gloss instead of matte)

The goal is a generation pass that yields 3 finished hero images suitable for marketing on the first or second try.
